introSection: Apocalypse is a steel roller coaster located at Lunapark in Agde, Occitanie, France. It has previously operated in the United Kingdom at Spanish City Amusement Park and subsequently at Flamingo Land.

sections.title: History
sections.content: The roller coaster first opened at Spanish City Amusement Park in 1983. It was called Corkscrew and was the second of two Corkscrew with bayerncurve installations to open in the United Kingdom, the first being Corkscrew at Alton Towers, which opened in 1980. Corkscrew arrived at Flamingo Land in 1990. It was situated in the Thunder Mountain Canyon area which in later years became the Plaza area. It was dismantled after the 2011 season, and the plot where it stood remained vacant until Sik was constructed in 2020, which opened in 2022. Following a repaint and a new sign to match its new name, Apocalypse opened at Lunapark on June 22, 2012.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Throughout its history, the ride has had white track and blue supports. It has a double corkscrew element.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: When the ride first opened in 1983, it had two trains built by Arrow Dynamics, however only one is present at Lunapark. The ride has a transfer track.

sections.title: Images
sections.content: Pictures of the roller coaster at Flamingo Land.

fullText:
Apocalypse is a steel roller coaster located at Lunapark in Agde, Occitanie, France. It has previously operated in the United Kingdom at Spanish City Amusement Park and subsequently at Flamingo Land.

The roller coaster first opened at Spanish City Amusement Park in 1983. It was called Corkscrew and was the second of two Corkscrew with bayerncurve installations to open in the United Kingdom, the first being Corkscrew at Alton Towers, which opened in 1980. Corkscrew arrived at Flamingo Land in 1990. It was situated in the Thunder Mountain Canyon area which in later years became the Plaza area. It was dismantled after the 2011 season, and the plot where it stood remained vacant until Sik was constructed in 2020, which opened in 2022. Following a repaint and a new sign to match its new name, Apocalypse opened at Lunapark on June 22, 2012.



Throughout its history, the ride has had white track and blue supports. It has a double corkscrew element.

When the ride first opened in 1983, it had two trains built by Arrow Dynamics, however only one is present at Lunapark. The ride has a transfer track.

Pictures of the roller coaster at Flamingo Land.
